title: Chapter 1 — Introduction to Datasets
objectives: Define a dataset as curated records and attributes prepared for a purpose
Classify datasets by structure, temporal behavior, and storage format
Assess quality along accuracy, completeness, consistency, relevance, and timeliness
Apply a first-pass exploration mindset before modeling
Recognize how organizations put datasets to work and why management matters

clips.title: Introduction to datasets
clips.bridge_in: This chapter starts from the most basic question: what counts as a dataset? This first clip defines records and attributes, then shows how the same idea appears in CSV tables and JSON documents—the vocabulary every later clip builds on.

clips.title: Types of datasets
clips.bridge_in: With records and attributes defined, the next question is how datasets are organized. This clip classifies structure (structured, unstructured, semi-structured) and temporal behavior (static versus dynamic)—the taxonomy used throughout the book.
clips.split_reason: Section 1.2 is too long for one 10-minute clip; formats (sec:1.2.5) are covered in clip-03.

clips.title: Dataset formats
clips.bridge_in: Structure tells you how records are organized; format tells you how they are stored on disk. This clip matches common formats—CSV, SQL, HDF5, GeoJSON—to the structure types from the previous clip.
clips.split_reason: Continuation of sec:1.2 after types; keeps clip under the 10-minute cap.

clips.title: Characteristics of good datasets
clips.bridge_in: Knowing a file's type and format is not enough—values must also be trustworthy for the task. This clip introduces five quality dimensions and shows how metadata and documentation make a dataset usable beyond the person who created it.

clips.title: Exploring a dataset
clips.bridge_in: Quality criteria tell you what good looks like; exploration is how you check whether a concrete file meets that bar. This clip covers why to explore first, which tools fit which tables, and a minimal pandas profiling pattern.

clips.title: Uses of datasets
clips.bridge_in: Definitions and exploration answer what a dataset is and how to read one. This clip answers where datasets create value—across sectors—and walks the customer-churn case study as an end-to-end analytics workflow.

clips.title: Dataset management
clips.bridge_in: Models and dashboards depend on quieter work: who may access the table, which version was trained, and how long personal fields may be retained. This final clip introduces management practices and privacy controls that keep datasets trustworthy over time.
